name: quantized

members.name: ELU
members.signature: ELU(scale: Tensor, zero_point: Tensor, qdtype: QDtype = quint8)
members.summary: Quantized `lucid.nn.ELU` — `elu(x, alpha)` then output fake-quant.
members.extended:
Carries the source module's `alpha` (the negative-branch scale
`alpha·(exp(x)-1)`), copied from the float `nn.ELU` in `from_float`.

members.name: Hardsigmoid
members.signature: Hardsigmoid(scale: Tensor, zero_point: Tensor, qdtype: QDtype = quint8)
members.summary: Quantized `lucid.nn.Hardsigmoid` — `hardsigmoid(x)` then fake-quant.
members.extended:
Like `Sigmoid` its output is bounded to `[0, 1]`, a natural
fixed-qparams range; the piecewise-linear gate of MobileNetV3's SE blocks.

members.name: Hardswish
members.signature: Hardswish(scale: Tensor, zero_point: Tensor, qdtype: QDtype = quint8)
members.summary: Quantized `lucid.nn.Hardswish` — `hardswish(x)` then output fake-quant.

members.name: LeakyReLU
members.signature: LeakyReLU(scale: Tensor, zero_point: Tensor, qdtype: QDtype = quint8)
members.summary: Quantized `lucid.nn.LeakyReLU` — `leaky_relu(x, slope)` then fake-quant.
members.extended:
Carries the source module's `negative_slope` (the slope applied to `x < 0`),
copied from the float `nn.LeakyReLU` in `from_float`.

members.name: Sigmoid
members.signature: Sigmoid(scale: Tensor, zero_point: Tensor, qdtype: QDtype = quint8)
members.summary: Quantized `lucid.nn.Sigmoid` — `sigmoid(x)` then output fake-quant.
members.extended:
The output is bounded to `[0, 1]`, so pairing this layer with a
`lucid.quantization.FixedQParamsObserver` (fixed `1/256` scale,
zero-point 0) in the qconfig skips calibrating a range that is known a priori.

members.name: Tanh
members.signature: Tanh(scale: Tensor, zero_point: Tensor, qdtype: QDtype = quint8)
members.summary: Quantized `lucid.nn.Tanh` — `tanh(x)` then output fake-quant.
members.extended:
The output is bounded to `[-1, 1]` (another fixed-qparams-friendly range);
converted from a calibrated float `nn.Tanh`.

members.name: Conv1d
members.signature: Conv1d(in_channels: int, out_channels: int, kernel_size: _IntTuple, stride: _IntTuple, padding: _IntTuple, dilation: _IntTuple, groups: int, bias: bool)
members.summary: Quantized 1-D convolution (int8 weight, dequantize-to-float compute).
members.extended:
Each forward dequantizes the per-output-channel int8 kernel, runs the
ordinary `F.conv1d`, then fake-quantizes the output to the calibrated
activation grid (the sidecar design-B numerics: int8 weight stored, but the
convolution itself runs in float so accuracy matches a real int8 kernel).
Produced from a calibrated float `lucid.nn.Conv1d` by
`lucid.quantization.convert` / `from_float`.

parameters.name: in_channels
parameters.description: Number of channels in the input signal.

parameters.name: out_channels
parameters.description: Number of channels produced by the convolution.

parameters.name: kernel_size
parameters.description: Size of the convolving kernel.

parameters.name: stride
parameters.description: Stride of the convolution.

parameters.name: padding
parameters.description: Implicit zero-padding added to both sides of the spatial axis.

parameters.name: dilation
parameters.description: Spacing between kernel elements.

parameters.name: groups
parameters.description: Number of blocked connections from input to output channels.

parameters.name: bias
parameters.description: Whether a (float) bias term is added after the convolution.

notes:
The weight is quantized **per-output-channel on axis 0** (much tighter than
per-tensor for wide kernels); the bias stays float. Only integer / tuple
padding with `padding_mode="zeros"` is supported — string `"same"` /
`"valid"` padding is deferred. See `lucid.nn.quantized.Conv2d`.

members.name: Conv2d
members.signature: Conv2d(in_channels: int, out_channels: int, kernel_size: _IntTuple, stride: _IntTuple, padding: _IntTuple, dilation: _IntTuple, groups: int, bias: bool)
members.summary: Quantized 2-D convolution (int8 weight, dequantize-to-float compute).
members.extended:
The workhorse of the quantized vision zoo. Each forward dequantizes the
per-output-channel int8 kernel, runs the ordinary `F.conv2d`, then
fake-quantizes the output to the calibrated activation grid (sidecar
design-B: int8 weight stored, but the convolution runs in float so accuracy
matches a real int8 kernel). Produced from a calibrated float
`lucid.nn.Conv2d` by `lucid.quantization.convert` /
`from_float`.

parameters.name: in_channels
parameters.description: Number of channels in the input image.

parameters.name: out_channels
parameters.description: Number of channels produced by the convolution.

parameters.name: kernel_size
parameters.description: Size of the convolving kernel.

parameters.name: stride
parameters.description: Stride of the convolution.

parameters.name: padding
parameters.description: Implicit zero-padding added to both sides of each spatial dim.

parameters.name: dilation
parameters.description: Spacing between kernel elements.

parameters.name: groups
parameters.description: Number of blocked connections from input to output channels.

parameters.name: bias
parameters.description: Whether a (float) bias term is added after the convolution.

notes:
The weight is quantized **per-output-channel on axis 0** (much tighter than
per-tensor for wide kernels); the bias stays float. Only integer / tuple
padding with `padding_mode="zeros"` is supported — string `"same"` /
`"valid"` padding is deferred.

members.name: Conv3d
members.signature: Conv3d(in_channels: int, out_channels: int, kernel_size: _IntTuple, stride: _IntTuple, padding: _IntTuple, dilation: _IntTuple, groups: int, bias: bool)
members.summary: Quantized 3-D convolution (int8 weight, dequantize-to-float compute).
members.extended:
The volumetric member of the quantized conv family (video / 3-D medical
models). Each forward dequantizes the per-output-channel int8 kernel, runs
the ordinary `F.conv3d`, then fake-quantizes the output to the calibrated
activation grid (sidecar design-B: int8 weight stored, but the convolution
runs in float so accuracy matches a real int8 kernel). Produced from a
calibrated float `lucid.nn.Conv3d` by
`lucid.quantization.convert` / `from_float`.

parameters.name: in_channels
parameters.description: Number of channels in the input volume.

parameters.name: out_channels
parameters.description: Number of channels produced by the convolution.

parameters.name: kernel_size
parameters.description: Size of the convolving kernel.

parameters.name: stride
parameters.description: Stride of the convolution.

parameters.name: padding
parameters.description: Implicit zero-padding added to both sides of each spatial dim.

parameters.name: dilation
parameters.description: Spacing between kernel elements.

parameters.name: groups
parameters.description: Number of blocked connections from input to output channels.

parameters.name: bias
parameters.description: Whether a (float) bias term is added after the convolution.

notes:
The weight is quantized **per-output-channel on axis 0** (much tighter than
per-tensor for wide kernels); the bias stays float. Only integer / tuple
padding with `padding_mode="zeros"` is supported — string `"same"` /
`"valid"` padding is deferred. See `lucid.nn.quantized.Conv2d`.

members.name: ConvTranspose1d
members.signature: ConvTranspose1d(in_channels: int, out_channels: int, kernel_size: _IntTuple, stride: _IntTuple, padding: _IntTuple, output_padding: _IntTuple, dilation: _IntTuple, groups: int, bias: bool)
members.summary: Quantized 1-D transposed convolution (fractionally-strided conv).
members.extended:
Each forward dequantizes the per-output-channel int8 kernel, runs
`F.conv_transpose1d`, then fake-quantizes the output to the calibrated
activation grid.  Produced from a calibrated float
`lucid.nn.ConvTranspose1d` by `lucid.quantization.convert`.

parameters.name: in_channels
parameters.description: Number of channels in the input signal.

parameters.name: out_channels
parameters.description: Number of channels produced by the transposed convolution.

parameters.name: kernel_size
parameters.description: Size of the convolving kernel.

parameters.name: stride
parameters.description: Stride of the transposed convolution (the upsampling factor).

parameters.name: padding
parameters.description: Implicit zero-padding subtracted from both sides of the spatial axis.

parameters.name: output_padding
parameters.description: Extra size added to one side of the output to disambiguate its shape.

parameters.name: dilation
parameters.description: Spacing between kernel elements.

parameters.name: groups
parameters.description: Number of blocked connections from input to output channels.

parameters.name: bias
parameters.description: Whether a (float) bias term is added after the convolution.

notes:
The transposed-weight layout `(in, out/groups, k)` makes a per-output-
channel axis ambiguous, so the weight is quantized **per-channel on axis 1**
(the output-channel axis) — much tighter than per-tensor for wide kernels.

members.name: ConvTranspose2d
members.signature: ConvTranspose2d(in_channels: int, out_channels: int, kernel_size: _IntTuple, stride: _IntTuple, padding: _IntTuple, output_padding: _IntTuple, dilation: _IntTuple, groups: int, bias: bool)
members.summary: Quantized 2-D transposed convolution (fractionally-strided conv).
members.extended:
The upsampling counterpart of `lucid.nn.quantized.Conv2d`, common in
segmentation decoders and GAN generators.  Each forward dequantizes the
per-output-channel int8 kernel, runs `F.conv_transpose2d`, then
fake-quantizes the output to the calibrated activation grid.  Produced from a
calibrated float `lucid.nn.ConvTranspose2d` by
`lucid.quantization.convert`.

parameters.name: in_channels
parameters.description: Number of channels in the input image.

parameters.name: out_channels
parameters.description: Number of channels produced by the transposed convolution.

parameters.name: kernel_size
parameters.description: Size of the convolving kernel.

parameters.name: stride
parameters.description: Stride of the transposed convolution (the upsampling factor).

parameters.name: padding
parameters.description: Implicit zero-padding subtracted from both sides of each spatial dim.

parameters.name: output_padding
parameters.description: Extra size added to one side of each output spatial dim.

parameters.name: dilation
parameters.description: Spacing between kernel elements.

parameters.name: groups
parameters.description: Number of blocked connections from input to output channels.

parameters.name: bias
parameters.description: Whether a (float) bias term is added after the convolution.

notes:
The weight is quantized **per-channel on axis 1** (output channels of the
`(in, out/groups, kh, kw)` layout) — the reference default, and much
tighter than per-tensor for wide kernels.

members.name: ConvTranspose3d
members.signature: ConvTranspose3d(in_channels: int, out_channels: int, kernel_size: _IntTuple, stride: _IntTuple, padding: _IntTuple, output_padding: _IntTuple, dilation: _IntTuple, groups: int, bias: bool)
members.summary: Quantized 3-D transposed convolution (fractionally-strided conv).
members.extended:
The volumetric upsampling layer (video / 3-D medical decoders).  Each forward
dequantizes the per-output-channel int8 kernel, runs `F.conv_transpose3d`,
then fake-quantizes the output to the calibrated activation grid.  Produced
from a calibrated float `lucid.nn.ConvTranspose3d` by
`lucid.quantization.convert`.

parameters.name: in_channels
parameters.description: Number of channels in the input volume.

parameters.name: out_channels
parameters.description: Number of channels produced by the transposed convolution.

parameters.name: kernel_size
parameters.description: Size of the convolving kernel.

parameters.name: stride
parameters.description: Stride of the transposed convolution (the upsampling factor).

parameters.name: padding
parameters.description: Implicit zero-padding subtracted from both sides of each spatial dim.

parameters.name: output_padding
parameters.description: Extra size added to one side of each output spatial dim.

parameters.name: dilation
parameters.description: Spacing between kernel elements.

parameters.name: groups
parameters.description: Number of blocked connections from input to output channels.

parameters.name: bias
parameters.description: Whether a (float) bias term is added after the convolution.

notes:
The weight is quantized **per-channel on axis 1** (output channels of the
`(in, out/groups, kd, kh, kw)` layout) — much tighter than per-tensor.

members.signature: FloatFunctional()

methods.name: add
methods.summary: Observed `x + y` (a residual skip-add).

methods.name: mul
methods.summary: Observed `x * y`.

methods.name: add_relu
methods.summary: Observed `relu(x + y)` — the fused residual-add of a ResNet block.

methods.name: cat
methods.summary: Observed concatenation along `dim`.

methods.name: add_scalar
methods.summary: Observed `x + scalar`.

methods.name: mul_scalar
methods.summary: Observed `x * scalar`.

members.summary: Float-domain quantizable merge ops; `prepare` observes their output.
members.extended:
Drop into a model in place of raw `x + y` / `lucid.cat` so the merge
result is calibrated.  Until `prepare` attaches an `activation_post_process`
observer these stay plain float ops (the `_obs` passthrough).

members.signature: QFunctional(scale: Tensor, zero_point: Tensor, qdtype: QDtype = quint8)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(scale: Tensor, zero_point: Tensor, qdtype: QDtype = quint8)

methods.name: add
methods.summary: Quantized `x + y`.

methods.name: mul
methods.summary: Quantized `x * y`.

methods.name: add_relu
methods.summary: Quantized `relu(x + y)`.

methods.name: cat
methods.summary: Quantized concatenation along `dim`.

methods.name: add_scalar
methods.summary: Quantized `x + scalar`.

methods.name: mul_scalar
methods.summary: Quantized `x * scalar`.

methods.name: from_float
methods.summary: Build from a calibrated `FloatFunctional` (reads its observer).

members.summary: Quantized element-wise merges — each result fake-quantized to one grid.
members.extended:
The converted form of `FloatFunctional`, produced by `convert`.  It
exposes the same `add` / `mul` / `cat` / `add_relu` / `*_scalar`
methods, but each fake-quantizes its output to the `(scale, zero_point)`
the `FloatFunctional` observed during calibration — so a residual add
or concat is requantized to a single consistent grid rather than left at
full precision.

members.name: ConvReLU1d
members.signature: ConvReLU1d(in_channels: int, out_channels: int, kernel_size: _IntTuple, stride: _IntTuple, padding: _IntTuple, dilation: _IntTuple, groups: int, bias: bool)
members.summary: Quantized 1-D convolution with a fused ReLU (int8 weight, float compute).
members.extended:
Behaves exactly like `lucid.nn.quantized.Conv1d` but applies ReLU to
the output *before* it is fake-quantized — matching calibration, where the
fused module's activation observer saw the post-ReLU range. Produced from a
calibrated float / QAT `ConvReLU1d` by `lucid.quantization.convert`
/ `from_float`.

parameters.name: kernel_size
parameters.description: Size of the convolving kernel.

parameters.name: stride
parameters.description: Stride of the convolution.

parameters.name: padding
parameters.description: Implicit zero-padding added to both sides of the spatial axis.

parameters.name: dilation
parameters.description: Spacing between kernel elements.

parameters.name: groups
parameters.description: Number of blocked connections from input to output channels.

parameters.name: bias
parameters.description: Whether a (float) bias term is added before the ReLU.

notes:
Constructor is inherited unchanged from `lucid.nn.quantized.Conv1d`;
only the post-op activation hook differs.

members.name: ConvReLU2d

members.summary: Quantized 2-D convolution with a fused ReLU (int8 weight, float compute).
members.extended:
The most common fused block in the quantized vision zoo. Behaves exactly
like `lucid.nn.quantized.Conv2d` but applies ReLU to the output
*before* it is fake-quantized — matching calibration, where the fused
module's activation observer saw the post-ReLU range. Produced from a
calibrated float / QAT `ConvReLU2d` by `lucid.quantization.convert`
/ `from_float`.

Behaves exactly like `lucid.nn.quantized.Linear` but applies ReLU
to the output *before* it is fake-quantized — matching calibration, where
the fused module's activation observer saw the post-ReLU range. Produced
from a calibrated float / QAT `LinearReLU` by
`lucid.quantization.convert` / `from_float`.

`True`.

notes:
Constructor is inherited unchanged from `lucid.nn.quantized.Linear`;
only the post-op activation hook differs.

members.name: Linear
members.signature: Linear(in_features: int, out_features: int, bias: bool = True)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(in_features: int, out_features: int, bias: bool = True)
methods.summary: Construct a Tensor from Python data, a NumPy array, or another Tensor.
methods.extended:
The data is funnelled through `lucid._factories.converters._to_impl`,
the canonical bridge between the outside world and Lucid's C++
`TensorImpl`. Python scalars become 0-d tensors; nested lists are
recursively flattened with shape inference; NumPy arrays cross the
single sanctioned host-to-engine boundary; and existing `Tensor`
sources are rewrapped (optionally with cast and/or device transfer).

parameters.name: data
parameters.annotation: ndarray | list | int | float | bool | Tensor
parameters.description:
Source data. Python scalars become 0-d tensors; lists are
recursively converted; NumPy arrays use the bridge boundary
documented in `lucid._factories.converters`; another Tensor
is shallow-copied to a new Tensor with possibly different
`dtype` / `device` / `requires_grad`.

parameters.name: dtype
parameters.description: Element type. If `None`, inferred from `data`.

parameters.name: device
parameters.description:
Target device (`"cpu"` or `"metal"`). If `None`, defaults
to the source device or `"cpu"` for host data.

parameters.name: requires_grad
parameters.description: Whether to record operations on this tensor for autograd.

returns.description:
A freshly constructed tensor whose storage is owned by the
engine.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> lucid.Tensor([1.0, 2.0, 3.0])
tensor([1., 2., 3.])
>>> lucid.Tensor([[1, 2], [3, 4]], dtype=lucid.int64).shape
(2, 2)
>>> x = lucid.Tensor(3.14, requires_grad=True)
>>> x.requires_grad
True
notes:
Constructor is one of the six sanctioned host-to-engine bridge
boundaries (rule **H4**). NumPy arrays cross the bridge exactly
once here; afterwards the data is owned by Lucid's engine. The
resulting layout is C-contiguous row-major:



$$
\text{stride}[i] = e \cdot \prod_{j=i+1}^{d-1} s_j
$$

where $e$ is the element size in bytes and $s_j$ are
the dimension sizes.

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Dequantize the weight, run linear, fake-quantize the output.

methods.name: from_float
methods.summary: Quantize a calibrated float `lucid.nn.Linear`.

methods.name: extra_repr
methods.summary: Override to add extra repr info (e.g. Linear shows in_features, etc.).

members.summary: Quantized linear (fully-connected) layer — int8 weight, float compute.
members.extended:
Under the sidecar representation (design B) the weight is stored as int8
codes plus per-output-channel `scale` / `zero_point` buffers; each
forward dequantizes the weight to float, runs the ordinary `F.linear`,
then fake-quantizes the output to the calibrated activation grid. This
yields the *numerics* of int8 inference (accuracy matches a real int8
kernel) while the GEMM itself stays in float. Produced from a calibrated
float `lucid.nn.Linear` by `lucid.quantization.convert` /
`from_float`.

parameters.name: in_features
parameters.description: Size of each input sample.

parameters.name: out_features
parameters.description: Size of each output sample.

parameters.name: bias
parameters.description:
Whether a (float) bias term is added after the linear map. Defaults to
`True`.

notes:
The weight is quantized **per-output-channel on axis 0**; the bias stays
float. For the real low-precision Metal GEMM (weight-only int4/int8, no
dequantize hop) see `lucid.nn.quantized.QuantizedLinearMLX`.

members.name: QuantizedLinearMLX
members.signature: QuantizedLinearMLX(in_features: int, out_features: int, bias: bool = True, bits: int = 8, group_size: int = 64, relu: bool = False)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(in_features: int, out_features: int, bias: bool = True, bits: int = 8, group_size: int = 64, relu: bool = False)

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Run the MLX low-precision GEMM `x @ packed_wᵀ` (+ bias, +ReLU).
methods.extended:
The kernel is Metal-only, so a CPU-carried activation is moved onto the
GPU for the GEMM and the result is moved **back to the input's device**.
That keeps this layer device-transparent — it accelerates inside an
otherwise-CPU model without forcing the whole model (or residual skip
branches) onto Metal.

methods.name: from_float
methods.signature: from_float(mod: nn.Module, bits: int = 8, group_size: int = 64, relu: bool = False)
methods.summary: Quantize a float `Linear`'s weight into MLX packed form (on Metal).

members.summary: Weight-only int4/int8 linear backed by a real Metal low-precision GEMM.
members.extended:
Unlike `lucid.nn.quantized.Linear` (which dequantizes the weight to
float and runs a float matmul), this layer stores the weight in MLX's
group-wise packed format and runs the genuine low-precision kernel
(`quantized_matmul`) — the actual compute *and* memory win. Build it from
a float `lucid.nn.Linear` via `from_float`, choosing `bits`
(4 or 8).

parameters.name: in_features
parameters.description: Size of each input sample.

parameters.name: out_features
parameters.description: Size of each output sample.

parameters.name: bias
parameters.description:
Whether a (float) bias term is added after the GEMM. Defaults to
`True`.

parameters.name: bits
parameters.description: Weight bit-width — `4` or `8`. Defaults to `8`.

parameters.name: group_size
parameters.description:
Number of input-dim elements sharing one `scale` / `bias` block in
the packed layout. Defaults to `64`.

parameters.name: relu
parameters.description:
If `True`, a ReLU is fused after the GEMM (+ bias). Defaults to
`False`.

examples:
>>> import lucid.nn as nn
>>> from lucid.nn.quantized import QuantizedLinearMLX
>>> lin = nn.Linear(512, 256)
>>> qlin = QuantizedLinearMLX.from_float(lin, bits=8)
>>> x = lucid.randn(4, 512)
>>> y = qlin(x)          # GEMM runs on Metal, y returns on x's device
>>> y.shape
(4, 256)
notes:
The kernel is **Metal-only** but the layer is device-transparent: a
CPU-carried activation is moved onto the GPU for the GEMM and the result is
moved **back to the input's device**, so this layer accelerates inside an
otherwise-CPU model without forcing the whole model onto Metal. The packed
weight is `(out, in·bits/32)` uint32 (tagged I32), with one `scale` +
`bias` per `group_size` block along the input dim.

members.name: DeQuantize
members.signature: DeQuantize()

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Identity (activations are already float-carried).

methods.name: from_float
methods.summary: Build from a `DeQuantStub` (no state to carry).

members.summary: Runtime exit from the quantized region — identity under design B.
members.extended:
The converted form of a `DeQuantStub`, produced by
`lucid.quantization.convert` / `from_float`. It marks where the
quantized region ends. Because Lucid's sidecar representation (design B)
carries activations as (fake-quantized) float throughout, there is nothing
to unpack: the forward is a plain identity and the module holds no state.
notes:
Takes no constructor arguments. It exists as an explicit boundary marker so
the quantized graph is symmetric with `Quantize` — useful for
tooling that walks the module tree.

members.name: DeQuantStub
members.signature: DeQuantStub(qconfig: object = None)

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Identity.

members.summary: Marks the quantized→float boundary in a to-be-quantized model.
members.extended:
The exit-side counterpart of `QuantStub`: place it where the
quantized region ends and float compute resumes. It is an identity at
float / calibration time; `lucid.quantization.convert` replaces it
with the runtime `DeQuantize` marker. Under Lucid's sidecar
representation (design B) activations are carried as (fake-quantized) float
throughout, so this marker never changes tensor values.

parameters.name: qconfig
parameters.description:
Quantization config attached to this boundary. When `None` (the
default) no `qconfig` attribute is set and the module inherits the
surrounding model's config during `prepare` / `convert`.

members.name: Quantize
members.signature: Quantize(scale: Tensor, zero_point: Tensor, qdtype: QDtype = quint8)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(scale: Tensor, zero_point: Tensor, qdtype: QDtype = quint8)

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Quantize `x` to the calibrated activation grid (float-carried).
methods.extended:
Integer inputs (e.g. token indices) are passed through unchanged —
only real-valued activations are quantized.

methods.name: from_float
methods.summary: Build from a calibrated `QuantStub` (reads its observer).

members.summary: Runtime entry into the quantized region — fake-quantizes the input.
members.extended:
The converted form of a calibrated `QuantStub`, produced by
`lucid.quantization.convert` / `from_float`. Each forward
quantizes a real-valued activation to the calibrated `(scale,
zero_point)` grid (integer inputs such as token indices are passed through
unchanged). Under the sidecar representation (design B) the result is
carried as fake-quantized float, so it has int8 numerics while staying a
float tensor.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Per-tensor activation scale from the calibrating observer.

parameters.name: zero_point
parameters.description: Per-tensor activation zero-point from the calibrating observer.

parameters.name: qdtype
parameters.description:
Quantized dtype whose `quant_min` / `quant_max` bound the grid.
Defaults to `lucid.quantization.quint8`.

members.name: QuantStub
members.signature: QuantStub(qconfig: object = None)

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Identity for PTQ (a hook observes); fake-quant for QAT.
methods.extended:
In QAT `prepare_qat` attaches a `FakeQuantize` as
`activation_post_process` — apply it so the input is fake-quantized
during training.  In PTQ it's a bare observer fed by a forward hook,
so the stub stays an identity.

members.summary: Marks the float→quantized boundary in a to-be-quantized model.
members.extended:
Identity at float / calibration time; `prepare` attaches an activation
observer and `convert` replaces it with `Quantize`.

members.name: QuantWrapper
members.signature: QuantWrapper(module: nn.Module)

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Quantize the input, run the wrapped model, dequantize the output.

members.summary: Wrap an arbitrary float model with `QuantStub` / `DeQuantStub`.
members.extended:
Lets a model that has no explicit quantization boundaries (e.g. a stock
zoo model) be quantized: the input is quantized on entry and dequantized
on exit, with the wrapped model in between.

members.name: Embedding
members.signature: Embedding(num_embeddings: int, embedding_dim: int, padding_idx: int | None = None)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(num_embeddings: int, embedding_dim: int, padding_idx: int | None = None)
methods.summary: Initialise the MSELoss module. See the class docstring for parameter semantics.

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Dequantize the table and look up `x`.

methods.name: from_float
methods.summary: Quantize a float `lucid.nn.Embedding`'s table (per-row int8).

methods.name: extra_repr
methods.summary: Return a string representation of the layer's configuration.

members.summary: Quantized embedding lookup table — per-row int8, dequantize-on-lookup.
members.extended:
The table is stored as int8 codes with a per-row (per-token) `scale` /
`zero_point`, which is the dominant memory win for large-vocabulary
embeddings. Each forward dequantizes the table and performs the ordinary
`lucid.nn.functional.embedding` lookup. Produced from a calibrated
float `lucid.nn.Embedding` by `lucid.quantization.convert` /
`from_float`.

parameters.name: num_embeddings
parameters.description: Size of the vocabulary (number of rows in the table).

parameters.name: embedding_dim
parameters.description: Dimensionality of each embedding vector.

parameters.name: padding_idx
parameters.description:
If given, the embedding at this index is treated as padding (its row is
not accumulated into gradients upstream). Defaults to `None`.

notes:
The table is quantized **per-row on axis 0** (one `scale` per token) with
a symmetric qint8 grid. When the source module carries no `qconfig`, a
default per-channel qint8 observer is used to calibrate the rows.

members.name: EmbeddingBag
members.signature: EmbeddingBag(num_embeddings: int, embedding_dim: int, mode: str = 'mean', padding_idx: int | None = None)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(num_embeddings: int, embedding_dim: int, mode: str = 'mean', padding_idx: int | None = None)
methods.summary: Initialise the Embedding module. See the class docstring for parameter semantics.

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, offsets: Tensor | None = None)
methods.summary: Dequantize the table and run the pooled bag lookup.

methods.name: from_float
methods.summary: Quantize a float `lucid.nn.EmbeddingBag`'s table (per-row int8).

methods.name: extra_repr
methods.summary: Return a string representation of the layer's configuration.

members.summary: Quantized `EmbeddingBag` — per-row int8 table, pooled dequant lookup.
members.extended:
Like `lucid.nn.quantized.Embedding`, the table is stored as int8
with a per-row (per-token) `scale`; each forward dequantizes the table
and runs the pooled bag lookup, reducing each bag of indices to a single
vector by `mode`. Produced from a calibrated float
`lucid.nn.EmbeddingBag` by `lucid.quantization.convert` /
`from_float`.

parameters.name: num_embeddings
parameters.description: Size of the vocabulary (number of rows in the table).

parameters.name: embedding_dim
parameters.description: Dimensionality of each embedding vector.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description:
Pooling reduction applied over each bag of gathered rows. Defaults to
`"mean"`.

parameters.name: padding_idx
parameters.description:
If given, the embedding at this index is treated as padding. Defaults
to `None`.

notes:
The table is quantized **per-row on axis 0** (one `scale` per token) with
a symmetric qint8 grid. When the source module carries no `qconfig`, a
default per-channel qint8 observer is used to calibrate the rows.

extended:
The int8-weight counterparts of the float `lucid.nn` layers, produced by
`lucid.quantization.convert` from a calibrated model.  Under the
sidecar representation (design B) they store int8 weights + `scale` /
`zero_point` buffers and compute in float (dequantize → op →
fake-quantize); the real low-precision GEMM is swapped in at Phase 6.

Also holds the boundary markers `QuantStub` / `DeQuantStub` placed in a
float model and their runtime forms `Quantize` / `DeQuantize`.
